title: Define a group-company relationship
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to define a group-company relationship that links multiple related companies (subsidiaries, sister concerns, or affiliated entities), so that related legal entities can be represented and managed together in the platform.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am authorized to manage the group-company construct, when I create a group-company relationship, then I can add multiple related companies (subsidiaries, sister concerns, affiliated entities) to it as members.; Given a group-company relationship exists, when I view it, then all linked companies are listed as members of the group with their relationship type.; Given companies are not explicitly added to a group-company construct, when the group is evaluated, then those companies are not treated as related and remain isolated.; Given I attempt to define a group-company relationship, when I am not authorized, then the action is denied and no relationship is created.; Given a group-company construct supports multiple related companies, when I add or remove member companies, then the membership updates accordingly and is reflected across dependent features.
source: FR 6.3.1 (definition of group-company relationships with multiple related companies)

title: Enable shared administration across related companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to enable shared administrative scenarios across the related companies in a group, so that administration can be handled centrally for affiliated entities.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group-company construct links related companies, when shared administration is explicitly authorized and configured, then an authorized admin can perform administrative actions across the linked companies.; Given shared administration is not explicitly authorized, when an admin attempts a cross-company administrative action, then the action is denied.; Given shared administration is enabled, when a cross-company administrative action is performed, then it is captured in the audit trail with actor, target company, and timestamp.; Given shared administration applies only to companies within the group-company construct, when an admin targets a company outside the construct, then the action is not permitted.
source: FR 6.3.2 (shared administrative scenarios; subject to explicit authorization and auditability)

title: Share locations across related companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to share locations across related companies within the group, so that affiliated entities can reuse common physical locations without duplicating them.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies are linked via the group-company construct, when location sharing is explicitly authorized and enabled, then a shared location is available to the authorized related companies.; Given location sharing is not explicitly authorized, when a company attempts to use another company's location, then access is not granted.; Given a location is shared across the group, when it is used or accessed by a related company, then the activity is recorded in the audit trail.; Given location sharing is scoped to the group-company construct, when a company outside the construct attempts to use a shared location, then access is denied.
source: FR 6.3.2 (shared locations across related companies; subject to explicit authorization and auditability)

title: Require explicit authorization for shared scenarios
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want shared administration and shared locations to require explicit authorization, so that cross-company sharing never happens implicitly or by default.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group-company construct exists, when no explicit authorization has been granted, then shared administration and shared location capabilities remain disabled by default.; Given explicit authorization is granted, when the shared scenario is used, then only the authorized companies and admins can act across the group.; Given authorization is revoked, when a previously authorized admin or company attempts a cross-company action, then the action is denied.; Given authorization changes are made, when the change is applied, then the authorization event itself is auditable.
source: FR 6.3.2 (subject to explicit authorization)

title: Maintain auditability of shared scenarios
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want all shared administrative and shared-location activity to be auditable, so that cross-company actions can be traced and reviewed for accountability.
acceptance_criteria: Given shared administration or shared locations are used across the group, when an action occurs, then it is recorded in an audit trail.; Given an auditor reviews cross-company activity, when they inspect the audit trail, then they can identify who performed the action, on which company, and when.; Given the audit trail is maintained, when cross-company actions accumulate, then no cross-company shared-scenario action is omitted from the trail.; Given audit records exist, when reviewed, then they are tamper-evident and retained for accountability.
source: FR 6.3.2 (auditability)

title: Enable consolidated reporting and cross-company directory search
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want consolidated reporting and cross-company directory search across linked companies, so that I can view unified information spanning the related entities.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies are explicitly linked via the group-company construct, cross-company access is explicitly enabled, and I hold an explicit group-level role, when I run consolidated reporting or a directory search, then results span the linked companies.; Given any one of the three prerequisites (explicit link, enabled cross-company access, granted group-level role) is not met, when I attempt consolidated reporting or cross-company directory search, then access is denied.; Given consolidated reporting or directory search runs, when results are returned, then row-level security filters restrict data to what I am authorized to see.; Given a consolidated report or cross-company search is executed, when it completes, then the access is recorded in the audit trail.
source: FR 6.3.3 (consolidated reporting and cross-company directory search prerequisites)

title: Grant explicit group-level roles for cross-company access
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to grant explicit group-level roles to users, so that only appropriately privileged users can access cross-company reporting and directory features.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user has not been granted a group-level role, when they attempt cross-company access, then the request is rejected.; Given a user is granted an explicit group-level role and cross-company access is enabled, when they access group features, then they can perform consolidated reporting and cross-company directory search.; Given a group-level role is granted or revoked, when the change is applied, then the change is auditable and takes effect on subsequent access attempts.; Given group-level roles govern access, when a user's role is insufficient for a specific action, then row-level security still constrains the data they can see.
source: FR 6.3.3 (users granted explicit group-level roles)

title: Enforce security constraints on cross-company access
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the system to enforce row-level security filters and maintain an audit trail for all cross-company access, so that group-company data access remains scoped and traceable.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user performs cross-company reporting or directory search, when results are returned, then row-level security filters restrict data to what the user is authorized to see.; Given any cross-company access occurs, when the access is processed, then an audit trail entry is recorded for it.; Given row-level security is enforced platform-wide, when a group-company feature bypasses application logic, then the row-level filters still constrain returned data at the data layer.; Given the audit trail captures all cross-company access, when a Platform Admin reviews it, then every cross-company read/report/search event is present with actor, company scope, and timestamp.
source: FR 6.3.3 (row-level security filters; audit trail for all cross-company access)

title: Establish and govern group-company constructs across the portfolio
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to establish which companies in my portfolio are linked into group-company constructs, so that related-entity relationships are governed at the portfolio level before shared scenarios are enabled.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer a portfolio of companies, when I link companies into a group-company construct, then only companies within my portfolio scope can be added as members.; Given a group-company construct is established, when I review the portfolio, then I can see which companies are related and which shared scenarios are authorized for each construct.; Given I am not authorized at the portfolio level, when I attempt to create or modify a group-company construct, then the action is denied.; Given group-company constructs are governed at the portfolio level, when a construct is created or changed, then the change is auditable.
source: FR 6.3.1 (definition of group-company relationships); FR 6.3.2 (subject to explicit authorization)

title: Participate in group-company shared scenarios as a member company
user_story: As a Company Admin of a member company, I want to see and consent to the shared administration and shared locations that apply to my company within a group-company construct, so that I retain visibility and control over cross-company access affecting my company.
acceptance_criteria: Given my company is a member of a group-company construct, when shared administration or shared locations are authorized for it, then I can view which cross-company capabilities apply to my company.; Given a cross-company admin action or shared-location access affects my company, when it occurs, then it is recorded in the audit trail visible to me for my company.; Given my company has not authorized a shared scenario, when another company's admin attempts a cross-company action on my company, then the action is denied.; Given my company is removed from the group-company construct, when the removal takes effect, then previously shared administration and locations no longer apply to my company.
source: FR 6.3.2 (shared administrative scenarios and shared locations across related companies; explicit authorization and auditability)

title: Search the cross-company directory within authorized scope
user_story: As an Employee (User) with an explicit group-level role, I want to search the directory across the linked companies in my group, so that I can find colleagues in related entities without seeing data outside my authorization.
acceptance_criteria: Given I hold an explicit group-level role and cross-company access is enabled for my group, when I search the cross-company directory, then results span the linked companies within my authorized scope.; Given I do not hold a group-level role or cross-company access is not enabled, when I attempt a cross-company directory search, then results are limited to my own company only.; Given I run a cross-company directory search, when results are returned, then row-level security filters restrict the records to those I am authorized to see.; Given I perform a cross-company directory search, when it executes, then the access is recorded in the audit trail.
source: FR 6.3.3 (cross-company directory search; explicit group-level roles; row-level security; audit trail)

title: Maintain effective-dated group-company membership with history and integrity
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want group-company memberships to be persisted as effective-dated, tenant-scoped records with full history, so that when companies join or leave a group over time the relationship remains accurate and auditable at any point in time.
acceptance_criteria: Given I add or remove a member company, when the change is saved, then the membership record is stored with an effective date and the prior state is retained as history rather than overwritten.; Given I query the group as of a given date, when the query runs, then it returns exactly the members that were effective in the construct on that date.; Given a company is already a member of a construct, when I attempt to add the same company again to the same construct, then a uniqueness/integrity constraint prevents the duplicate membership.; Given all group-company records are tenant-scoped, when membership data is persisted or read, then it is isolated to the owning tenant and never leaks across tenants.
source: FR 6.3.1 (L1 domain and data: effective-dated, tenant-scoped membership records with history and integrity constraints)

title: Govern cross-company access and shared-scenario settings as versioned config
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want the cross-company access enablement, shared-administration, and shared-location authorizations to be governed as versioned, effective-dated configuration, so that these settings can be changed without code and their history is preserved.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group-company construct exists, when I set the cross-company access toggle and shared-scenario authorizations, then they are stored as per-tenant governed configuration that the platform engines read at evaluation time.; Given I change a configuration value, when the change is saved, then a new version is created with an effective date and the prior version is retained.; Given a configuration change has a future effective date, when that date is reached, then the new setting takes effect automatically without a code deployment.; Given configuration governs cross-company behavior, when a setting is added, changed, or removed, then the configuration change is auditable with actor, prior value, new value, and timestamp.
source: FR 6.3.3 (L2 config: cross-company access enablement as governed, versioned, effective-dated configuration); FR 6.3.2 (L2 config: shared-scenario authorizations)

title: Evaluate cross-company access prerequisites via the Rules engine
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the shared Rules engine to evaluate the cross-company access prerequisites from configuration as a decision, so that consolidated reporting and directory search are gated consistently by the platform runtime rather than per-feature code.
acceptance_criteria: Given the cross-company access prerequisites (explicit group link, cross-company access enabled, granted group-level role) are defined in configuration, when a user requests a consolidated report or cross-company directory search, then the Rules engine evaluates all three conditions before access is granted.; Given all three prerequisites evaluate true, when the engine returns its decision, then the request is permitted and proceeds under row-level security.; Given any one prerequisite evaluates false, when the engine returns its decision, then the request is denied with the failing condition identified.; Given the prerequisite configuration is updated, when the engine next evaluates a request, then it uses the current configuration without a code change.
source: FR 6.3.3 (L3 rules engine: evaluation of consolidated-reporting and directory-search prerequisites)

title: Manage group-company constructs through a metadata-driven admin screen
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven screen to create constructs, manage member companies, and configure shared scenarios, so that I can govern group-company relationships through the self-service UI without back-office intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the group-company management screen, when it loads, then I see the constructs I am authorized for, their member companies with relationship types, and the shared scenarios authorized for each.; Given I use the screen to add or remove members or toggle shared-administration, shared-location, and cross-company access, when I submit, then the changes are applied and reflected on the screen and in dependent features.; Given I am not authorized for a construct or action, when I view the screen, then the corresponding controls are hidden or disabled.; Given a cross-company directory search or consolidated report is available to me, when I invoke it from the screen, then results are constrained by row-level security and the access is recorded in the audit trail.
source: FR 6.3.1 (L4 presentation: metadata-driven construct management screen); FR 6.3.2 (L4 presentation: shared-scenario configuration UI)

title: Create a group-company structure with required attributes and auto-generated code
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to create a group-company structure capturing its name, type, member companies, and group administrator, so that affiliated legal entities are formally represented with a unique identifier.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a Platform Admin (the only role permitted to create groups per §7.1), when I create a group-company structure, then I must provide a unique Group Name of 3-100 characters, a Group Type, at least 2 active member companies, and a Group Administrator who holds the Group Company Administrator role.; Given I submit a valid group, when it is created, then the system auto-generates a GroupCode in the format GROUP-YYYY-NNN (YYYY = year of creation, NNN = sequential number) and sets Status to Active.; Given a group name already exists, when I attempt to reuse it, then creation is rejected because Group Name must be unique across the platform.; Given a group is created, when the operation completes, then a GROUP_CREATED audit event is recorded capturing the group structure, member companies, and creator.
source: GROUP-FR-001 (§3.3.1 group company definition); §4.2.4 GroupCompany entity; §8 GROUP_CREATED

title: Select the group type from the supported enum
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to classify each group as Holding, Sister, or JointVenture, so that the relationship semantics between member companies are explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create or edit a group, when I set the Group Type, then I may choose only from the enum Holding, Sister, or JointVenture.; Given Group Type is Holding, when I save, then the structure represents a parent-subsidiary relationship.; Given Group Type is Sister, when I save, then member companies are treated as related companies at the same level with no parent.; Given Group Type is JointVenture, when I save, then the structure represents a partnership among member companies.; Given a value outside the enum is submitted, when it is validated, then it is rejected.
source: GROUP-FR-003 (§3.3.1 group types); §4.2.4 (GroupType enum Holding/Sister/JointVenture)

title: Require a parent company for Holding structures
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the parent company to be captured for Holding-type groups, so that the parent-subsidiary hierarchy is unambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: Given Group Type is Holding, when I create or save the group, then a Parent Company reference is required (ParentCompanyId is conditional per §4.2.4).; Given Group Type is Sister or JointVenture, when I create the group, then Parent Company is optional and not applicable.; Given a Holding group is saved without a parent company, when it is validated, then the save is rejected with a message indicating a parent company is required for Holding structures.; Given a parent company is set, when it is chosen, then it must be one of the active member companies of the group.
source: GROUP-FR-001 (§3.3.1 parent company for holding company structures); §4.2.4 (ParentCompanyId conditional)

title: Enforce the one-group-per-company constraint
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a company to belong to only one group-company structure at a time, so that affiliation relationships remain unambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company is already a member of a group-company structure, when I attempt to add it to a second group, then the system rejects the action with error GROUP_001 (Company already in another group, HTTP 409).; Given a company belongs to no group, when I add it to a group, then the membership is created successfully.; Given I remove a company from its current group, when the removal is committed, then that company becomes eligible to join a different group.; Given the constraint is enforced at the data layer, when concurrent additions of the same company are attempted, then only one membership persists and the other fails with GROUP_001.
source: GROUP-FR-002 (§3.3.1 a company can belong to only one group); §9 GROUP_001

title: Restrict group membership to active companies with a minimum of two members
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want only active companies to be added to a group and require at least two members, so that groups represent valid, operational affiliated entities.
acceptance_criteria: Given I add a company to a group, when the company's status is not Active (e.g. Draft, Suspended, Inactive, or Archived), then the addition is rejected because group companies must be active.; Given I create a group, when fewer than 2 member companies are provided, then creation is rejected because a group requires 2+ active companies (§4.2.4).; Given an existing member company transitions out of Active status, when the change occurs, then the system flags the group per policy and prevents the inactive company from being used in new shared scenarios.; Given all provided members are Active and the count is 2 or more, when I save, then the group is created successfully.
source: GROUP-FR-002 (§3.3.1 group companies must be active companies); §4.2.4 (2+ active companies)

title: Prevent circular references in group structures
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the system to block circular ownership references, so that group hierarchies stay acyclic and valid.
acceptance_criteria: Given company A owns or parents company B in a group, when I attempt to configure B to own or parent A, then the system rejects it with error GROUP_002 (Circular reference detected, HTTP 400).; Given a proposed membership or parent assignment would create any cycle across the ownership graph, when it is validated, then the operation is denied before persistence.; Given a valid acyclic structure, when I save, then no circular-reference error is raised and the structure persists.; Given a circular reference is attempted, when it is rejected, then the failing relationship is identified in the error response.
source: GROUP-FR-002 (§3.3.1 circular references not allowed: A owns B, B cannot own A); §9 GROUP_002

title: Manage member companies of an owned group
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to add and remove member companies within the group I administer, so that I can maintain the group composition without platform intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Given I hold the Group Company Administrator role for a group, when I manage that group, then I can add or remove member companies and update the group (per §7.1 Manage Group and Add/Remove Members are permitted for the Group Company Admin of the own group only).; Given I attempt to manage a group I do not administer, when the action is checked, then it is denied.; Given I add a member company, when the membership record is created, then it captures JoinDate and AddedBy (§4.2.5 GroupCompanyMember) and enforces the one-group-per-company (GROUP_001) and active-company constraints.; Given I remove a member company, when I confirm, then the membership is ended and the change is recorded in the audit trail.
source: GROUP-FR-001 (§3.3.1 member companies); §4.2.5 GroupCompanyMember junction; §7.1 (Manage Group, Add/Remove Members)

title: Share policy templates across group companies without auto-propagation
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to share a policy from one company as a read-only template so each member company can create its own instance, so that standardization is enabled without overriding company-level control.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy is created in one company, when I share it with the group, then it is exposed to member companies as a read-only template.; Given a member company adopts a shared template, when it does so, then it creates its own independent policy instance from the template.; Given the source template is later changed, when the change is saved, then the changes do not propagate automatically to previously created company instances.; Given a company has created an instance, when it edits its instance, then those edits affect only that company and never the shared template or other companies' instances.
source: GROUP-FR-005 (§3.3.2 shared policy templates: read-only template, per-company instance, no automatic propagation)

title: Enforce the shared-location permission matrix
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want shared locations to follow a strict owner and referencing-company permission matrix, so that only the owning company controls a location while others reference it with approval.
acceptance_criteria: Given a location, when actions are attempted, then only the owning company can Create and Edit it, while a referencing company cannot (§3.3.2 permission matrix).; Given a company wants to reference another company's location, when it requests the reference, then the reference is allowed only with explicit approval by the location owner, and viewing is limited to details such as address and contact info.; Given a referenced location, when either the owner or a referencing company assigns it to an employee, then use in employee assignment is permitted for both.; Given a referencing company no longer needs a shared location, when it removes the reference, then removal is permitted for the referencing company and recorded in the audit trail, and the owner's location is unaffected.
source: GROUP-FR-004 (§3.3.2 shared location permissions matrix)

title: Produce consolidated group reporting outputs
user_story: As a Group Company Admin holding the Group Reporting Viewer role, I want consolidated reports across the group, so that I can view aggregated headcount, org structure, and leave/attendance for the affiliated entities.
acceptance_criteria: Given I hold the Group Reporting Viewer role, when I run consolidated reporting, then I can view aggregated headcount across the group, a cross-company organization chart view, and consolidated leave/attendance summaries.; Given I do not hold the Group Reporting Viewer role, when I attempt consolidated group reporting, then access is denied.; Given consolidated reports run, when results are returned, then row-level security restricts data to what I am authorized to see and the access is recorded in the audit trail.; Given consolidated reporting spans member companies, when a company is not a member of the group, then its data is excluded from the aggregates.
source: GROUP-FR-006 (§3.3.2 consolidated reporting for group companies; Group Reporting Viewer role)

title: Restrict group creation and management by role
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want group creation restricted to Platform Admins and group management restricted to the owning Group Company Admin, so that RBAC for group-company structures is enforced.
acceptance_criteria: Given the RBAC matrix (§7.1), when a user attempts to create a group, then only a Platform Admin is permitted while Portfolio Admin, Group Company Admin, and Company Admin are denied.; Given a group exists, when management or add/remove-members is attempted, then only the Group Company Admin of that group (own) or a Platform Admin is permitted.; Given a Company Admin or Portfolio Admin attempts any group create, manage, or member action, when authorization is checked, then it is denied with an authorization error such as AUTH_001.; Given a permitted role performs a group action, when it completes, then the action is recorded in the audit trail.
source: §7.1 (Group Company RBAC rows: Create Group, Manage Group, Add/Remove Members)
